questions.prompt: Your one-line prompt ('Summarize this article') is producing mediocre output. What's the most reliable fix?

choices.text: Switch to a larger / more expensive model.
Rewrite the prompt with specifics: role, audience, tone, length, format, and 1–2 examples of what good output looks like. Specificity beats model upgrades far more often than people realize.
Add 'PLEASE BE THOROUGH!!!' to the prompt.

questions.explanation: The single most common reason for mediocre output is a vague prompt. Specificity (role, audience, tone, length, format) plus a couple of examples typically matches or exceeds the quality you'd get from a model upgrade — at a fraction of the cost.

questions.prompt: Which of these is genuinely supported by OpenAI's prompt engineering guidance?

choices.text: Adding 'You are a world-class expert' to every prompt.
Providing 1–3 representative examples of the format and style you want — often called 'few-shot prompting.'
Threatening the model with negative consequences.

questions.explanation: Examples are one of the highest-leverage prompting patterns. One or two labeled examples of the expected output often outperforms several paragraphs of description. The other options are folklore — they don't measurably help on modern models.
